jokes.text: I'm scaling up like a goddamn boa constrictor over here.
jokes.setup: Dee complaining about psoriasis to her therapist
jokes.punchline: Using 'scaling up' to describe both skin flaking and snake behavior
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - medical condition, visual imagery, and character voice. Economy is excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: I'm scaling up like a goddamn boa constrictor over here.

jokes.text: Well, you're not a skin doctor, so let's drop it already, okay?
jokes.setup: Therapist trying to explore deeper meaning behind psoriasis
jokes.punchline: Dee dismissively shutting down therapeutic insight
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Dee's resistance to self-reflection and therapy in general.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Well, you're not a skin doctor, so let's drop it already, okay?

jokes.text: screaming and tears and physical threats, uh, Frank pulled out his gun, somebody dropped the 'N' bomb
jokes.setup: Therapist asking what happened at the cooperative dinner
jokes.punchline: Casual recounting of increasingly extreme chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure building to absurd extremes, delivered with characteristic Sunny casual darkness.

dialogue.line: screaming and tears and physical threats, uh, Frank pulled out his gun, somebody dropped the 'N' bomb

jokes.text: Oh. No one was assigned the dishes.
jokes.setup: Therapist asking why the dinner 'fell apart' after extreme chaos
jokes.punchline: Mundane dish assignment as the root cause
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - builds to extreme chaos then reveals hilariously mundane cause. Perfect economy and structure.

dialogue.line: Oh. No one was assigned the dishes.

jokes.text: And that all happened over the dishes?
jokes.setup: Therapist trying to process the dish revelation
jokes.punchline: Disbelief at the disproportionate response
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man reaction that highlights the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Therapist
dialogue.line: And that all happened over the dishes?

jokes.text: Who's going to do the dishes! And also, this is a great opportunity for you to see how insane my asshole friends are.
jokes.setup: Therapist asking what needs to be resolved
jokes.punchline: Dee's dual agenda of dishes and proving friends are crazy
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Dee's manipulative nature and lack of self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Who's going to do the dishes! And also, this is a great opportunity for you to see how insane my asshole friends are.

jokes.text: Of course I brought them here... what, are you gonna come to my house?
jokes.setup: Therapist surprised Dee brought her friends
jokes.punchline: Dee's entitled expectation that therapist should accommodate her
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Dee's entitlement and obliviousness to professional boundaries.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Of course I brought them here... what, are you gonna come to my house?

jokes.text: Yeah, actually, this argument is more about determining who the loser will be, because most of us will be winners.
jokes.setup: Therapist saying arguments don't have winners
jokes.punchline: Dennis's twisted logic about multiple winners and one loser
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dennis logic - narcissistic and mathematically absurd but delivered with complete confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, actually, this argument is more about determining who the loser will be, because most of us will be winners.

jokes.text: Setting the table? Are you dumb? I hosted!
jokes.setup: Mac claiming he had the hardest job setting the table
jokes.punchline: Dee's outrage and elevation of 'hosting' as supreme difficulty
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dee's need to be seen as the hardest worker despite minimal effort.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Setting the table? Are you dumb? I hosted!

jokes.text: It was wall-to-wall details! I was on edge the whole time.
jokes.setup: Dee defending the difficulty of hosting
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overstatement about party planning stress
jokes.explanation: Solid character comedy showing Dee's tendency to dramatize minor inconveniences.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: It was wall-to-wall details! I was on edge the whole time.

jokes.text: The food was mush. / The food was divine. It was pheasant.
jokes.punchline: Mac's defensive response with fancy bird name
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between characters' perspectives and Mac's pretentious defense.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: The food was mush.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: The food was divine. It was pheasant.

jokes.text: Well, I had the hardest job of anyone... I traveled all the way to this exotic merchant in search of this rare fowl...
jokes.setup: Charlie claiming he had the hardest job
jokes.punchline: Grandiose description of grocery shopping
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie delusion - turning mundane task into epic quest with flowery language.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, I had the hardest job of anyone... I traveled all the way to this exotic merchant in search of this rare fowl...

jokes.text: You went to the corner gourmet grocery store and you bought pheasant.
jokes.setup: Charlie's grandiose description of his shopping quest
jokes.punchline: Mac's deflating reality check
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncturing of Charlie's delusion with simple, brutal reality.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You went to the corner gourmet grocery store and you bought pheasant.

jokes.text: I have a background in academic psych myself, from an Ivy League college, no less, not this, uh, LaSalle.
jokes.setup: Dennis offering to help the therapist
jokes.punchline: Snobbish dismissal of therapist's credentials
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis arrogance - immediately establishing superiority over the professional.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I have a background in academic psych myself, from an Ivy League college, no less, not this, uh, LaSalle.

jokes.text: It sounds like a pasta dish.
jokes.punchline: Frank's crude comparison to food
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Frank humor - reducing institution to food comparison.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It sounds like a pasta dish.

jokes.text: Some people don't have the same access that we do.
jokes.punchline: Dennis's faux-progressive condescension
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dennis move - appearing magnanimous while being deeply condescending.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Some people don't have the same access that we do.

jokes.text: big group dealio
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-professional language mixed with casual slang
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis character moment mixing fake expertise with casual language.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: big group dealio

jokes.text: I hosted the shit out of that party!
jokes.setup: Mac explaining why he's on edge
jokes.punchline: Aggressive overstatement about hosting duties
jokes.explanation: Good Mac intensity about relatively minor achievement.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I hosted the shit out of that party!

jokes.text: sometimes I feel like they don't even understand me and we're not even that good of friends.
jokes.punchline: Honest moment of insecurity
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Mac vulnerability that makes what follows funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: sometimes I feel like they don't even understand me and we're not even that good of friends.

jokes.text: (screams) That's bullshit! We're, like, the best friends in the whole goddamn world! Goddamn them for making me think otherwise!
jokes.punchline: Violent swing to opposite extreme
jokes.explanation: Brilliant emotional whiplash - from vulnerability to rage in seconds, perfectly Mac's instability.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: (screams) That's bullshit! We're, like, the best friends in the whole goddamn world! Goddamn them for making me think otherwise!

jokes.text: I was just thinking of something that Charlie said that was really funny.
jokes.punchline: Awkward explanation for random outburst
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Mac's inability to regulate emotions or social cues.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I was just thinking of something that Charlie said that was really funny.

jokes.text: I gained and lost 60 pounds in three months.
jokes.punchline: Impossible timeline delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Absurd claim delivered with complete sincerity, perfect Mac delusion.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I gained and lost 60 pounds in three months.

jokes.text: Well, first of all, through God, all things are possible, so jot that down.
jokes.setup: Therapist saying the weight loss is 'almost impossible'
jokes.punchline: Mac's religious justification with commanding tone
jokes.explanation: Iconic Mac line - perfect blend of religious delusion, condescension, and absurd authority. Became cultural touchstone.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, first of all, through God, all things are possible, so jot that down.

jokes.text: people used to cross the street when I would walk by. They'd be, like, 'Whoa, look at that monster coming towards us, you know, barreling towards us.'
jokes.punchline: Imagined dramatic public reactions
jokes.explanation: Good Mac fantasy about being intimidating, 'barreling' is great word choice.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: people used to cross the street when I would walk by. They'd be, like, 'Whoa, look at that monster coming towards us, you know, barreling towards us.'

jokes.text: Lady, I was as big as a skyscraper, and now I'm as tiny as a postage stamp.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining his size perception issues
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous scale comparisons
jokes.explanation: Great visual metaphors showing Mac's completely distorted self-perception.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Lady, I was as big as a skyscraper, and now I'm as tiny as a postage stamp.

jokes.text: You leave this pen here and people are supposed to think, 'Wait, that looks like a dick.'
jokes.punchline: Calling out obvious psychological trick
jokes.explanation: Solid visual gag recognition and Mac's blunt assessment of therapeutic tools.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You leave this pen here and people are supposed to think, 'Wait, that looks like a dick.'

jokes.text: Dennis is, like, my best buddy
jokes.setup: Mac talking about Dennis giving him 'size pills'
jokes.punchline: Describing manipulative drug dealer as 'best buddy'
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Mac's loyalty to someone actively harming him.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dennis is, like, my best buddy

jokes.text: So if we talk about it, then you think that I'll get most bigger... from talking!
jokes.punchline: Mac thinking talking will literally make him physically bigger
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Mac logic - completely misunderstanding therapy as literal physical transformation.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: So if we talk about it, then you think that I'll get most bigger... from talking!

jokes.text: when you started talking about God, I realized that you're one of the smart ones.
jokes.punchline: Basing intelligence assessment on religious mention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac - judging professional competence based on perceived religious alignment.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: when you started talking about God, I realized that you're one of the smart ones.

jokes.text: Get out, slim. Let's go. / Ticktock, ticktock. Time's up. Ticktock, tiny man.
jokes.punchline: Condescending size references and time pressure
jokes.explanation: Dennis being manipulative controller, using Mac's size insecurity against him.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Get out, slim. Let's go.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Ticktock, ticktock. Time's up. Ticktock, tiny man.

jokes.text: I taped the knob... I knew I'd be popping in and out quite a bit.
jokes.setup: Therapist noting door should be locked
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual admission of sabotage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis control freak behavior - premeditated session disruption.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I taped the knob... I knew I'd be popping in and out quite a bit.

jokes.text: Fine, I'll do the dishes! You're just gonna make me do 'em anyway!
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately assuming he's been set up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie paranoia and victimization complex on full display.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Fine, I'll do the dishes! You're just gonna make me do 'em anyway!

jokes.text: basement stuff, cleaning urinals, uh, blood stuff, your basic slimes, your sludges. Anything dead, or decay, you know, I'm on it
jokes.setup: Therapist asking what Charlie work is
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disgusting catalog delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of gross tasks, Charlie's casual pride in disgusting work.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: basement stuff, cleaning urinals, uh, blood stuff, your basic slimes, your sludges. Anything dead, or decay, you know, I'm on it

jokes.text: I love the dark, I love slippery things, I love being naked, uh, in the sewer.
jokes.setup: Charlie saying he loves Charlie work at its core
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disturbing list of preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie - finding genuine joy in things that would horrify normal people.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I love the dark, I love slippery things, I love being naked, uh, in the sewer.

jokes.text: Bleach smells good, uh, tastes good
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of bleach consumption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Charlie treating poison as gourmet experience.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Bleach smells good, uh, tastes good

jokes.text: I'm, like, probably the weirdest guy in the universe, you know. Probably even weirder than someone from Saturn.
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to hypothetical alien life
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie logic - using imaginary aliens as reference point for normalcy.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm, like, probably the weirdest guy in the universe, you know. Probably even weirder than someone from Saturn.

jokes.text: would it be weird if you survived an abortion?
jokes.setup: Charlie asking what would be weird
jokes.punchline: Casually dropping extremely dark personal detail
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - treating traumatic backstory as casual conversation topic.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: would it be weird if you survived an abortion?

jokes.text: you shared a bed with a man who may or may not be your father?
jokes.punchline: Another disturbing family situation presented casually
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of disturbing family dynamics, uncertainty makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: you shared a bed with a man who may or may not be your father?

jokes.text: If you eat cat food to go to sleep, and you have such a fascination with cats that maybe you glue cat hair on the back of your neck every now and then?
jokes.punchline: Specific bizarre behavior involving cat obsession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie specificity - cat food as sleep aid and hair gluing shows perfect character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: If you eat cat food to go to sleep, and you have such a fascination with cats that maybe you glue cat hair on the back of your neck every now and then?

jokes.text: so if I got, like, more skin or something, then I could, like, never do a bad thing in my life
jokes.setup: Therapist saying being comfortable in your skin is good
jokes.punchline: Charlie taking it completely literally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - misunderstanding metaphor as literal prescription for more skin.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: so if I got, like, more skin or something, then I could, like, never do a bad thing in my life

jokes.text: You're saying, like, do the things you do, go even further with them. You know, maybe get a ton of cat hair, glue it all over your body, you know, walk around like a cat man in the night through the alleyways.
jokes.punchline: Escalating to full cat transformation fantasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie escalation - taking minor quirk to logical extreme of becoming cat person.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You're saying, like, do the things you do, go even further with them. You know, maybe get a ton of cat hair, glue it all over your body, you know, walk around like a cat man in the night through the alleyways.

jokes.text: And stop hiding the pigeon. This one. You know, let him be who he wants, let him... let him fly away. Go!
jokes.setup: Charlie pulling something from his jacket
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's been carrying a pigeon around
jokes.explanation: Great visual reveal and Charlie's attempt at therapeutic bird release.

dialogue.line: And stop hiding the pigeon. This one. You know, let him be who he wants, let him... let him fly away. Go!

jokes.text: He's dead, I think.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to release the pigeon
jokes.punchline: Casual realization the bird is dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - matter-of-fact delivery of morbid revelation.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He's dead, I think.

jokes.text: I think I might have hugged him a little too hard, kind of smothered him a bit
jokes.punchline: Admitting to accidentally killing it with affection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie - causing harm through misguided love and care.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I think I might have hugged him a little too hard, kind of smothered him a bit

jokes.text: I ain't talking about nothing! This skull is Fort Knox.
jokes.setup: Therapist trying to get Frank to open up
jokes.punchline: Frank declaring his head impenetrable
jokes.explanation: Good Frank defiance with colorful metaphor about mental fortification.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I ain't talking about nothing! This skull is Fort Knox.

jokes.text: Got me so scrambled up. Next thing you know, I was shanghaied upstate to a nitwit school.
jokes.punchline: Revealing institutionalization as a child
jokes.explanation: Good backstory reveal explaining Frank's issues, 'shanghaied' is great word choice.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Got me so scrambled up. Next thing you know, I was shanghaied upstate to a nitwit school.

jokes.text: Not just for nuts in the head. Bodies, too. Back then, science was real crude. They stuck us all together.
jokes.punchline: Revealing it mixed mental and physical disabilities
jokes.explanation: Good period detail about institutional care, sets up frog kid reveal.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Not just for nuts in the head. Bodies, too. Back then, science was real crude. They stuck us all together.

jokes.text: My roommate was a frog kid. You ever see a frog kid?
jokes.punchline: Introducing concept of 'frog kid'
jokes.explanation: Great concept creation - 'frog kid' becomes memorable phrase, casual delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: My roommate was a frog kid. You ever see a frog kid?

jokes.text: Got my first kiss there.
jokes.punchline: Unexpected romantic memory from same place
jokes.explanation: Brilliant tonal shift - from horror to sweetness in perfect economy.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Got my first kiss there.

jokes.text: She was an angel. Always smiling. That's because she had no lips, but her mouth was still very much in play.
jokes.setup: Frank describing his first love romantically
jokes.punchline: Revealing physical deformity and sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sunny tone - mixing genuine sentiment with disturbing details and crude humor.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: She was an angel. Always smiling. That's because she had no lips, but her mouth was still very much in play.

jokes.text: She died two weeks later. She thought she was a space man with a plastic bag for a helmet.
jokes.setup: Frank getting emotional about his lost love
jokes.punchline: Revealing she suffocated herself with delusion
jokes.explanation: Devastating dark comedy - tragic death caused by mental illness described matter-of-factly.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: She died two weeks later. She thought she was a space man with a plastic bag for a helmet.

jokes.text: Oh, you unzipped me. It's all coming back. I hate you!
jokes.punchline: Blaming therapist for emotional breakthrough
jokes.explanation: 'Unzipped' is brilliant metaphor for psychological breakdown, Frank's anger at vulnerability.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, you unzipped me. It's all coming back. I hate you!

jokes.text: How quickly you rattled their cages, broke them all down.
jokes.punchline: Viewing therapy as manipulation rather than help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis sociopathy - seeing emotional breakdown as entertainment.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: How quickly you rattled their cages, broke them all down.

jokes.text: It's like the thrill of being near the executioner's switch, knowing that at any moment, you could throw it, but knowing you never will. But you could.
jokes.setup: Dennis describing the thrill of psychological control
jokes.punchline: Comparing therapy to execution with sinister undertone
jokes.explanation: Chilling Dennis monologue revealing his sociopathic nature through elaborate metaphor.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's like the thrill of being near the executioner's switch, knowing that at any moment, you could throw it, but knowing you never will. But you could.

jokes.text: He's always sucking on the pens in our apartment. I'm always having to hide them.
jokes.punchline: Revealing ongoing domestic pen-hiding situation
jokes.explanation: Great detail about their living situation and Dennis managing Mac's quirks.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He's always sucking on the pens in our apartment. I'm always having to hide them.

jokes.text: My file on Dee was started in the second grade.
jokes.punchline: Revealing decades-long psychological surveillance of his sister
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal of Dennis's lifelong sociopathic behavior and sibling obsession.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: My file on Dee was started in the second grade.

jokes.text: So they're Mexican ephedra. Calling them size pills was an eloquent solution.
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're dangerous diet drugs with fake marketing
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of Dennis's manipulation and his pride in deceptive marketing.

dialogue.line: So they're Mexican ephedra. Calling them size pills was an eloquent solution.

jokes.text: Drawing conclusions. And... drawing this.
jokes.setup: Therapist asking what Dennis is writing
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'drawing' while showing crude sketch
jokes.explanation: Simple pun with visual element showing Dennis's juvenile side.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Drawing conclusions. And... drawing this.

jokes.text: Were you or were you not... the first choice for the female lead in The Notebook?
jokes.punchline: Calling out specific ridiculous lie
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Dee's delusional acting claims and therapeutic confrontation.

dialogue.speaker: Therapist
dialogue.line: Were you or were you not... the first choice for the female lead in The Notebook?

jokes.text: I graciously handed it to Rachel McAdams and she did a fine job with the film. I'm secure enough to admit that.
jokes.setup: Dee claiming she was first choice for The Notebook
jokes.punchline: Magnanimous tone about 'giving' role to actual star
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee delusion - not just claiming the role but being generous about giving it up.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: I graciously handed it to Rachel McAdams and she did a fine job with the film. I'm secure enough to admit that.

jokes.text: (Brooklyn accent): Oh, what do you want to hear, Doc, huh? That I had cigarettes put out on me when I was a kid? That this isn't a surgery scar, huh? That my father stabbed me? Huh?
jokes.punchline: Fake traumatic backstory in bad accent
jokes.explanation: Good Dee performance trying to manufacture sympathy with fake trauma.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: (Brooklyn accent): Oh, what do you want to hear, Doc, huh? That I had cigarettes put out on me when I was a kid? That this isn't a surgery scar, huh? That my father stabbed me? Huh?

jokes.text: You're speaking right now with a Brooklyn accent and yet... (normal voice): Boston. It was a Boston accent.
jokes.punchline: Dee incorrectly defending her accent choice
jokes.explanation: Good Dee stubbornness and bad acting being called out professionally.

dialogue.speaker: Therapist
dialogue.line: You're speaking right now with a Brooklyn accent and yet...

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: (normal voice): Boston. It was a Boston accent.

jokes.text: Oh, really? Uh... ta-da! I've been lying the whole time! Oh! So, good, you admitted it. Uh-uh, that was acting. What I just... right there was acting. I said I was lying, and that wasn't even true. That was acting.
jokes.setup: Therapist wanting to see Dee act
jokes.punchline: Confusing layers of deception and performance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dee spiral of lies about lying, perfect character confusion.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, really? Uh... ta-da! I've been lying the whole time! Oh! So, good, you admitted it. Uh-uh, that was acting. What I just... right there was acting. I said I was lying, and that wasn't even true. That was acting.

jokes.text: Tell me I'm good. Tell me it was good. Tell me I'm good. Tell me that was good. Tell me I'm good. Tell me I'm good... Tell me I'm good.
jokes.punchline: Desperate repetitive plea for validation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee neediness and validation-seeking, escalating desperation.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Tell me I'm good. Tell me it was good. Tell me I'm good. Tell me that was good. Tell me I'm good. Tell me I'm good... Tell me I'm good.

jokes.text: Dennis, there was another twin in your mother's womb. We were going to call him Donnie. You and Deandra devoured him before he could be born. You gobbled him up.
jokes.punchline: Revealing cannibalistic twin absorption
jokes.explanation: Legendary Sunny moment - combining real medical phenomenon with Frank's dramatic delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Dennis, there was another twin in your mother's womb. We were going to call him Donnie. You and Deandra devoured him before he could be born. You gobbled him up.

jokes.text: You would've been the good one. It would've been Bruce's kid anyway, Frank.
jokes.punchline: Dennis revealing paternity issues while dismissing sentiment
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis callousness and family dysfunction reveal.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You would've been the good one. It would've been Bruce's kid anyway, Frank.

jokes.text: The therapist implied that God wanted me to have bovine hormones and I think she's going to get them for me.
jokes.punchline: Believing therapist will provide cow hormones via divine will
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac religious delusion and complete misunderstanding of therapy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: The therapist implied that God wanted me to have bovine hormones and I think she's going to get them for me.

jokes.text: How's that? Huh? You having a white Christmas? You having a white Christmas, you bitch?
jokes.punchline: Taunting victim with cocaine reference
jokes.explanation: Dennis violence with creative drug reference taunting.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: How's that? Huh? You having a white Christmas? You having a white Christmas, you bitch?

jokes.text: Yeah, I served you pigeon, okay? I'm not going to spend my money on a pheasant when I can get a perfectly good street bird.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's practical justification for using street birds
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic and callback to dead pigeon - frugal and disgusting.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I served you pigeon, okay? I'm not going to spend my money on a pheasant when I can get a perfectly good street bird.

jokes.text: Dee, do the (bleep) dishes.
jokes.punchline: Professional therapist breaking and swearing
jokes.explanation: Perfect climax - professional finally breaks under gang's pressure, resolving episode conflict.

dialogue.line: Dee, do the (bleep) dishes.

jokes.text: Boom! I feel like a weight has been lifted. You're a good therapist.
jokes.punchline: Mac interpreting breakdown as therapeutic success
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac misreading of situation - seeing therapeutic breakthrough in breakdown.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Boom! I feel like a weight has been lifted. You're a good therapist.

jokes.text: Here's some dishes. You screwed me, you bitch. There's the dishes, all really nice and clean.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic compliance with continued hostility
jokes.explanation: Good Dee resentful compliance showing her inability to be gracious even in defeat.

dialogue.speaker: Sweet Dee
dialogue.line: Here's some dishes. You screwed me, you bitch. There's the dishes, all really nice and clean.
